title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: corrgram package provides functions creating corrgrams using three different graphics systems, base, grid, lattice. Base R graphics + single function corrgram() dataframes matrices. + Enables features found paper Friendly (2002). - automatic legend. - easily combined graphics. lattice graphics + Separate panel functions lattice::levelplot() dataframes lattice::splom() correlation matrices. + Enables automatic legend. + Enables corrgrams conditioned variables. + Can combined lattice graphics complex figures. - feature complete compared base R. grid graphics + single function corrgram2() either dataframes correlation matrices. + Enables automatic legend. + Can combined grid graphics complex figures. - feature complete compared base R. + Faster base R evaluated inside Positron.

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: vignette demonstrates corrgrams base R graphics reproduces figures Friendly (2002).

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: data 11 measures performance salary 263 baseball players 1986 baseball season United States. data used 1988 Data Expo Joint Statistical Meetings. first 6 rows data upper-left corner correlation matrix given .

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: Figure 2 shows two ways graphically display correlation matrix using panel.shade() panel.pie() functions.
code: vars2 <- c("Assists","Atbat","Errors","Hits","Homer","logSal",            "Putouts","RBI","Runs","Walks","Years") corrgram(baseball[,vars2], order=TRUE,          main="Baseball data PC2/PC1 order",          lower.panel=panel.shade, upper.panel=panel.pie,          diag.panel=panel.minmax, text.panel=panel.txt)

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: Figure 3 shows eigenvector plot correlation matrix. forms basis orderings variables corrgram Figure 4b next section, beginning “AtBat” moving counter-clockwise “Years”. Note: Eigenvectors unique change sign.
code: baseball.cor <- cor(baseball[,vars2], use='pair') baseball.eig <- eigen(baseball.cor)$vectors[,1:2] e1 <- baseball.eig[,1] e2 <- baseball.eig[,2] plot(e1,e2,col='white', xlim=range(e1,e2), ylim=range(e1,e2)) text(e1,e2, rownames(baseball.cor), cex=1) title("Eigenvector plot of baseball data") arrows(0, 0, e1, e2, cex=0.5, col="red", length=0.1)

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: figure 4a variables sorted alphabetical order given data. figure 4b, variables sorted according principal component ordering Figure 3 look possible clustering variables. surprising see times bat strongly correlated higher number hits higher number runs.
code: corrgram(baseball[,vars2], main="Baseball data (alphabetic order)") corrgram(baseball[,vars2], order=TRUE,          main="Baseball data (PC order)",          panel=panel.shade, text.panel=panel.txt)

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: Figure 5 shows corrgram numeric variables dataframe. Non-numeric columns data ignored.
code: corrgram(baseball, order=TRUE, main="Baseball data (PC order)")

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: Figure 6 shows corrgram automotive data 74 different models cars 1979. two obvious groups variables Note, arrangement slightly different Friendly.
code: corrgram(auto, order=TRUE, main="Auto data (PC order)")

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: inverse correlation matrix expresses conditional dependence independence variables. variables sorted order figure 4. One example interpretation : controlling variables, still large correlation Years log Salary.
code: rinv <- function(r){   # r is a correlation matrix   # calculate r inverse and scale to correlation matrix   # Derived from Michael Friendly's SAS code    ri <- solve(r)   s <- diag(ri)   s <- diag(sqrt(1/s))   ri <- s %*% ri %*% s   n <- nrow(ri)   ri <- ri * (2*rep(1,n) - matrix(1, n, n))   diag(ri) <- 1  # Should already be 1, but could be 1 + epsilon   colnames(ri) <- rownames(ri) <- rownames(r)   return(ri) }  vars7 <- c("Years", "logSal", "Homer", "Putouts", "RBI", "Walks",            "Runs", "Hits", "Atbat", "Errors", "Assists") cb <- cor(baseball[,vars7], use="pair") corrgram(-rinv(cb), main=expression(paste("Baseball data ", R^-1)))

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: Figure 8 shows partial independence corrgram automotive data, Price MPG partialed .
code: require(Matrix) # For block diagonal function ## Loading required package: Matrix partial <- function(r, xvar){   # r is a correlation matrix   # Calculate partial correlation of y|x   yvar <- setdiff(colnames(r), xvar)   ri <- r[yvar,yvar] - r[yvar,xvar] %*% solve(r[xvar,xvar]) %*% r[xvar,yvar]   s <- diag(ri)   s <- diag(sqrt(1/s))   ri <- s %*% ri %*% s   ri <- as.matrix(Matrix::bdiag(ri, r[xvar, xvar]))   diag(ri) <- 1  # Should already be 1, but could be 1 + epsilon   colnames(ri) <- rownames(ri) <- c(yvar, xvar)   return(ri) }  vars8a <- c("Gratio", "Rep78", "Rep77", "Hroom", "Trunk", "Rseat",             "Length", "Weight", "Displa", "Turn") vars8b <- c("MPG", "Price") vars8 <- c(vars8a, vars8b) auto.cor <- cor(auto[, vars8], use="pair") auto.par <- partial(auto.cor, vars8b) corrgram(auto.par,          lower.panel=panel.pie, upper.panel=panel.pie,          main="Auto data, partialing out Price,MPG")

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: Figure 11 provides another way display data, using ellipses loess lines. Long, narrow ellipses represent high correlations circular ellipses represent low correlations.
code: corrgram(baseball[,vars2], order=TRUE,          main="Baseball correlation ellipses",          panel=panel.ellipse,          text.panel=panel.txt, diag.panel=panel.minmax)

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: Demonstrate density panel, correlation confidence panel  Demonstrate panel.bar, panel.ellipse, panel.minmax, col.regions
code: corrgram(iris,          main="Iris data with example panel functions",          lower.panel=panel.pts, upper.panel=panel.conf,          diag.panel=panel.density) ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter corrgram(auto, order=TRUE,          main="Auto data (PC order)",          lower.panel=corrgram::panel.ellipse,          upper.panel=panel.bar, diag.panel=panel.minmax,          col.regions=colorRampPalette(c("darkgoldenrod4", "burlywood1",                                         "darkkhaki", "darkgreen")))

title: Examples for the corrgram package
text: example showing one way plot ratings data.
code: load(url("https://github.com/alexanderrobitzsch/sirt/blob/master/data/data.ratings3.rda?raw=true"))  # jitter first, so the upper/lower panels are symmetric data.ratings3 <- transform(data.ratings3,                            c2=jitter(crit2), c3=jitter(crit3),                            c4=jitter(crit4), c6=jitter(crit6))                             library(corrgram) panel.raters <- function (x, y, corr = NULL, col.regions, cor.method, ...) {   if (!is.null(corr))      return()   plot.xy(xy.coords(x, y), type = "p", ...)   abline(lm(y ~ x))   box(col = "lightgray") } corrgram(data.ratings3[,7:10], diag=panel.density, lower.panel=panel.raters, upper.panel=panel.conf) ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter

title: Finding the nearest proper correlation matrix
code: require(psych) ## Loading required package: psych cc2 <- psych::cor.smooth(cc) ## Warning in psych::cor.smooth(cc): Matrix was not positive definite, smoothing ## was done max(cc2) ## [1] 1

title: Finding the nearest proper correlation matrix
text: converting matrix valid correlation matrix, accurate corrgram can created:
code: library(sfsmisc) # nearcor uses 'identical' and says the matrix is not symmetric isSymmetric(cc) # TRUE ## [1] TRUE identical(cc, t(cc)) # FALSE ## [1] FALSE # round slightly to make it symmetric cc3 <- nearcor(round(cc,12))$cor max(cc3) ## [1] 1 require(corrgram) ## Loading required package: corrgram corrgram(cc2, lower=panel.cor) ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter ## Warning in par(usr): argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter

title: Examples of grid corrgrams
text: corrgram package provides functions creating corrgrams using three different graphics systems, base, grid, lattice. Base R graphics + single function corrgram() dataframes matrices. + Enables features found paper @friendly2002corrgrams. - automatic legend. - easily combined graphics. lattice graphics + Separate panel functions lattice::levelplot() dataframes lattice::splom() correlation matrices. + Enables automatic legend. + Enables corrgrams conditioned variables. + Can combined lattice graphics complex figures. - feature complete compared base R. grid graphics + single function corrgram2() either dataframes correlation matrices. + Enables automatic legend. + Can combined grid graphics complex figures. - feature complete compared base R. + Faster base R evaluated inside Positron.

title: Examples of grid corrgrams
text: vignette demonstrates create corrgrams using grid graphics corrgram2() function variety panel functions visualizing correlations different ways.

title: Examples of grid corrgrams
text: vignette demonstrates use grid-based panels corrgram2, provide flexible modern correlation matrix visualizations.

title: Examples of grid corrgrams
text: vote dataset contains roll call voting records US Senators. show grid-based correlation plot absolute correlations, ordering, legend.
code: corrgram2(vote, abs = TRUE, order = TRUE, legend = TRUE, title = "vote data")

title: Examples of grid corrgrams
text: auto dataset contains various automobile attributes. select subset numeric variables display grid-based correlation plot using fill panel.
code: vars6 <- setdiff(colnames(auto), c("Model", "Origin")) corrgram2(auto[, vars6],            lower.panel = grid_panel.shade, upper.panel=grid_panel.pie,           title = "auto data", legend = TRUE)

title: Examples of lattice corrgrams
text: corrgram package provides functions creating corrgrams using three different graphics systems, base, grid, lattice. Base R graphics + single function corrgram() dataframes matrices. + Enables features found paper Friendly (2002). - automatic legend. - easily combined graphics. lattice graphics + Separate panel functions lattice::levelplot() dataframes lattice::splom() correlation matrices. + Enables automatic legend. + Enables corrgrams conditioned variables. + Can combined lattice graphics complex figures. - feature complete compared base R. grid graphics + single function corrgram2() either dataframes correlation matrices. + Enables automatic legend. + Can combined grid graphics complex figures. - feature complete compared base R. + Faster base R evaluated inside Positron.

title: Examples of lattice corrgrams
text: vignette demonstrates create corrgrams using lattice graphics, can use custom panel functions provided corrgram package along lattice::splom() lattice::levelplot() functions. example type corrgram shown .

title: Examples of lattice corrgrams
text: levelplot() function lattice single plotting region, (default) suppot upper lower panels. However, can write custom panel function different glyphs diagonal. See panel.ellipse() example . Using splom() makes easy include color scale next corrgram.
code: library("lattice") library("corrgram") ##  ## Attaching package: 'corrgram' ## The following object is masked from 'package:lattice': ##  ##     panel.fill # The easiest way to have an automatic color key is to set the theme opar <- trellis.par.get() trellis.par.set(   regions=list(col=colorRampPalette(c("red","salmon","white","royalblue","navy"))) )  # Create a correlation matrix library(MASS) # foor Cars93 cor.Cars93 <-   cor(Cars93[, !sapply(Cars93, is.factor)], use = "pair") ord <- order.dendrogram(as.dendrogram(hclust(dist(cor.Cars93)))) cars93 <- cor.Cars93[ord,ord] head(cars93) ##                       RPM Rev.per.mile   MPG.city MPG.highway Passengers ## RPM             1.0000000    0.4947642  0.3630451   0.3134687 -0.4671376 ## Rev.per.mile    0.4947642    1.0000000  0.6958570   0.5874968 -0.3349756 ## MPG.city        0.3630451    0.6958570  1.0000000   0.9439358 -0.4168559 ## MPG.highway     0.3134687    0.5874968  0.9439358   1.0000000 -0.4663858 ## Passengers     -0.4671376   -0.3349756 -0.4168559  -0.4663858  1.0000000 ## Rear.seat.room -0.3421751   -0.3770096 -0.3843469  -0.3666844  0.6941337 ##                Rear.seat.room Luggage.room   Horsepower   Max.Price   Min.Price ## RPM                -0.3421751   -0.5248449  0.036688212  0.02501478 -0.04259816 ## Rev.per.mile       -0.3770096   -0.5927915 -0.600313870 -0.37402421 -0.47039499 ## MPG.city           -0.3843469   -0.4948936 -0.672636151 -0.54781090 -0.62287544 ## MPG.highway        -0.3666844   -0.3716291 -0.619043685 -0.52256074 -0.57996581 ## Passengers          0.6941337    0.6533166  0.009263668  0.05321592  0.06123644 ## Rear.seat.room      1.0000000    0.6519675  0.256731532  0.24725979  0.37664210 ##                       Price Turn.circle EngineSize      Width ## RPM            -0.004954931  -0.5056506 -0.5478978 -0.5397211 ## Rev.per.mile   -0.426395113  -0.7331596 -0.8240086 -0.7804604 ## MPG.city       -0.594562163  -0.6663889 -0.7100032 -0.7205344 ## MPG.highway    -0.560680362  -0.5936833 -0.6267946 -0.6403592 ## Passengers      0.057860074   0.4490247  0.3727212  0.4899786 ## Rear.seat.room  0.311498819   0.4663276  0.5027498  0.4656176 ##                Fuel.tank.capacity     Weight     Length  Wheelbase ## RPM                    -0.3333452 -0.4279315 -0.4412493 -0.4678123 ## Rev.per.mile           -0.6097098 -0.7352642 -0.6902333 -0.6368238 ## MPG.city               -0.8131444 -0.8431385 -0.6662390 -0.6671076 ## MPG.highway            -0.7860386 -0.8106581 -0.5428974 -0.6153842 ## Passengers              0.4720951  0.5532730  0.4852941  0.6940544 ## Rear.seat.room          0.5096887  0.5262505  0.5499578  0.6672586 # lattice corrgram using pie-shaped glyphs levelplot(cars93, xlab = NULL, ylab = NULL,           at = do.breaks(c(-1.01, 1.01), 101), panel = levelplot_panel.pie,           scales = list(x = list(rot = 90)), colorkey = list(space = "top") ) # lattice corrgram using ellipse-shaped glyphs above the diagonal # and value labels below the diagonal levelplot(cars93, xlab = NULL, ylab = NULL,           at = do.breaks(c(-1.01, 1.01), 101), panel = levelplot_panel.ellipse,           label=TRUE,           scales = list(x = list(rot = 90)), colorkey = list(space = "top") )

title: Examples of lattice corrgrams
text: Since lattice::splom() function supports conditioning factor, can use create corrgrams conditioned factor. penguins data provides nice example Simpson’s paradox, overall correlation two variables can negative, correlation within group (species) can positive.       can also use hexbin package create hexagonal binning plots upper panels scatterplot matrix. useful large number points want visualize density points different regions plot.
code: pengvars <- c("bill_len", "bill_dep", "flipper_len", "body_mass") library(lattice) splom(~penguins[ , pengvars], upper.panel=splom_panel.pie, pscales=0) splom(~penguins[ , pengvars]|penguins$species, upper.panel=splom_panel.pie, pscales=0) splom(~penguins[ , pengvars], upper.panel=splom_panel.shade, pscales=0) splom(~penguins[ , pengvars]|penguins$species, upper.panel=splom_panel.shade, pscales=0) splom(~penguins[ , pengvars], upper.panel=splom_panel.ellipse, pscales=0) splom(~penguins[ , pengvars]|penguins$species, upper.panel=splom_panel.ellipse, pscales=0) # Hexbin library(lattice) library(hexbin) splom(~penguins[ , pengvars], upper.panel=hexbin::panel.hexbinplot, pscales=0) splom(~penguins[ , pengvars]|penguins$species, upper.panel=hexbin::panel.hexbinplot, pscales=0)

title: Authors and Citation
text: Kevin Wright. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Wright K (2026). corrgram: Plot Correlogram. R package version 1.15, https://kwstat.github.io/corrgram/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {corrgram: Plot a Correlogram},   author = {Kevin Wright},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 1.15},   url = {https://kwstat.github.io/corrgram/}, }

title: Plot a Correlogram
text: Homepage: https://kwstat.github.io/corrgram Repository: https://github.com/kwstat/corrgram corrgram package provides simple way create correlograms raw data correlation matrix.

title: Plot a Correlogram
text: corrgram package provides functions creating corrgrams using three different graphics systems, base, grid, lattice. Base R graphics + single function corrgram() dataframes matrices. + Enables features found paper @friendly2002corrgrams. - automatic legend. - easily combined graphics. lattice graphics + Separate panel functions lattice::levelplot() dataframes lattice::splom() correlation matrices. + Enables automatic legend. + Enables corrgrams conditioned variables. + Can combined lattice graphics complex figures. - feature complete compared base R. grid graphics + single function corrgram2() either dataframes correlation matrices. + Enables automatic legend. + Can combined grid graphics complex figures. - feature complete compared base R. + Faster base R evaluated inside Positron.

title: Plot a Correlogram
code: # Install the released version from CRAN: install.packages("corrgram")  # Install the development version from GitHub: install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("kwstat/corrgram")

title: Plot a Correlogram
code: require(corrgram) # Base R corrgram(mtcars, order=TRUE, lower.panel=panel.shade, upper.panel=panel.pie,          text.panel=panel.txt, main="mtcars")

title: Plot a Correlogram
code: # grid vars6 <- setdiff(colnames(auto), c("Model", "Origin")) corrgram2(auto[, vars6],            lower.panel = grid_panel.shade, upper.panel=grid_panel.pie,           title = "auto data", legend = TRUE)

title: Plot a Correlogram
code: library(lattice) pengvars <- c("bill_len", "bill_dep", "flipper_len", "body_mass") splom(~penguins[ , pengvars]|penguins$species, upper.panel=splom_panel.pie,        pscales=0, main="penguins data in lattice")

title: Baseball Hitter's Data — baseball
code: vars2 <- c("Assists","Atbat","Errors","Hits","Homer","logSal",            "Putouts","RBI","Runs","Walks","Years") corrgram(baseball[,vars2],          lower.panel=panel.shade, upper.panel=panel.pie)

title: Draw a correlogram — corrgram
text: Note: `corrgram()` uses base R graphics. corrgram function produces graphical display correlation matrix, called correlogram.  cells matrix can shaded colored show correlation value. Automatic variable reordering can used improve visualization.

title: Draw a correlogram — corrgram
code: corrgram(   x,   type = NULL,   order = FALSE,   labels,   panel = panel.shade,   lower.panel = panel,   upper.panel = panel,   diag.panel = NULL,   text.panel = textPanel,   label.pos = c(0.5, 0.5),   label.srt = 0,   cex.labels = NULL,   row1attop = TRUE,   dir = "",   gap = 0,   abs = FALSE,   col.regions = colorRampPalette(c("red", "salmon", "white", "royalblue", "navy")),   cor.method = "pearson",   outer.labels = NULL,   ... )

title: Draw a correlogram — corrgram
text: x tall data frame one observation per row, correlation matrix. type Use 'data' 'cor'/'corr' explicitly specify 'x' data correlation matrix.  Rarely needed. order variables re-ordered? improve visualization placing similar variables next . Use TRUE "PCA" angular PCA-based re-ordering (see Friendly, 2002). 'seriation' package installed, distance-based method seriate can used. Examples "OLO" optimal leaf ordering, "GW", "HC", "MDS" (see Hahsler Buchta, 2008). labels Labels use (instead data frame variable names) diagonal panels. 'order' option used, vector labels also appropriately reordered function. panel Function used plot contents panel. lower.panel, upper.panel Separate panel functions used /diagonal. diag.panel, text.panel Panel function used diagonal. label.pos Horizontal vertical placement label diagonal panels. label.srt String rotation diagonal labels. cex.labels Graphics parameter diagonal panels. row1attop TRUE diagonal like " \ ", FALSE diagonal like " / ". dir Use dir="left" instead 'row1attop'. gap Distance panels. abs Use absolute value correlations clustering?  Default FALSE. col.regions function returning vector colors. cor.method Correlation method use panel functions.  Default 'pearson'.  Alternatives: 'spearman', 'kendall'. outer.labels list form 'list(bottom,left,top,right)'. 'bottom=TRUE' (example), variable labels added along bottom outside edge. control, use 'bottom=list(labels,cex,srt,adj)', 'labels' vector variable labels, 'cex' affects size, 'srt' affects rotation, 'adj' affects adjustment labels. Defaults: 'labels' uses column names; cex=1'; 'srt=90' (bottom/top), 'srt=0' (left/right); 'adj=1' (bottom/left), 'adj=0' (top/right). ... Additional arguments passed plotting methods.

title: Draw a correlogram — corrgram
text: correlation matrix used plotting returned. 'order' 'abs' arguments affect returned value.

title: Draw a correlogram — corrgram
text: Note: Use 'col.regions' argument specify colors. Non-numeric columns data ignored. -diagonal panels specified panel.pts, panel.pie, panel.shade, panel.fill, panel.bar, panel.ellipse, panel.conf. panel.cor. Diagonal panels specified panel.txt, panel.minmax, panel.density. Use NULL panel omit drawing panel. function basically modification pairs.default function use customized panel functions. panel.conf function uses cor.test calculates pearson correlations.  Confidence intervals available cor.test methods (kendall, spearman). can create panel functions starting one included panel functions making suitable modifications.  Note way panel functions called inside main function, custom panel function must include arguments shown panel.pts function, even custom panel function use arguments!

title: Draw a correlogram — corrgram
text: Friendly, Michael.  2002.  Corrgrams: Exploratory Displays Correlation Matrices.  American Statistician, 56, 316–324. http://datavis.ca/papers/corrgram.pdf D. J. Murdoch E. D. Chow. 1996. Graphical Display Large Correlation Matrices. American Statistician, 50, 178-180. Hahsler M, Hornik K, Buchta C. 2008. Getting things order: introduction R package seriation. Journal Statistical Software, 25(3), 1–34. ISSN 1548-7660, doi:10.18637/jss.v025.i03

title: Draw a correlogram — corrgram
text: Kevin Wright

title: Draw a correlogram — corrgram
code: # To reproduce the figures in Michael Friendly's paper, see the # vignette, or see the file 'friendly.r' in this package's # test directory.  # Demonstrate density panel, correlation confidence panel corrgram(iris, lower.panel=panel.pts, upper.panel=panel.conf,          diag.panel=panel.density) #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter   # Demonstrate panel.shade, panel.pie, principal component ordering vars2 <- c("Assists","Atbat","Errors","Hits","Homer","logSal",            "Putouts","RBI","Runs","Walks","Years") corrgram(baseball[vars2], order=TRUE, main="Baseball data PC2/PC1 order",          lower.panel=panel.shade, upper.panel=panel.pie)   # Use reordering options from package seriation (only available if #    package seriation is installed) if ("seriation" %in% rownames(installed.packages())) { corrgram(baseball[vars2], order= "OLO",           main="Baseball data with optimal leaf order",           lower.panel=panel.shade, upper.panel=panel.pie)  corrgram(baseball[vars2], order= "R2E",           main="Baseball data with rank 2 ellipse seriation",           lower.panel=panel.shade, upper.panel=panel.pie)  # the following seriation methods are available seriation::list_seriation_methods("dist") }   #>  [1] "ARSA"           "BBURCG"         "BBWRCG"         "Enumerate"      #>  [5] "GSA"            "GW"             "GW_average"     "GW_complete"    #>  [9] "GW_single"      "GW_ward"        "HC"             "HC_average"     #> [13] "HC_complete"    "HC_single"      "HC_ward"        "Identity"       #> [17] "MDS"            "MDS_angle"      "OLO"            "OLO_average"    #> [21] "OLO_complete"   "OLO_single"     "OLO_ward"       "QAP_2SUM"       #> [25] "QAP_BAR"        "QAP_Inertia"    "QAP_LS"         "R2E"            #> [29] "Random"         "Reverse"        "SGD"            "SPIN_NH"        #> [33] "SPIN_STS"       "Sammon_mapping" "Spectral"       "Spectral_norm"  #> [37] "TSP"            "VAT"            "isoMDS"         "isomap"         #> [41] "metaMDS"        "monoMDS"         # CAUTION: The latticeExtra package also has a 'panel.ellipse' function # that clashes with the same-named function in corrgram. In order to use # the right one, the example below uses 'lower.panel=corrgram::panel.ellipse'. # If you do not have latticeExtra loaded, you can just use # 'lower.panel=panel.ellipse'.  # Demonstrate panel.bar, panel.ellipse, panel.minmax, col.regions corrgram(auto, order=TRUE, main="Auto data (PC order)",          lower.panel=corrgram::panel.ellipse,          upper.panel=panel.bar, diag.panel=panel.minmax,          col.regions=colorRampPalette(c("darkgoldenrod4", "burlywood1",                                         "darkkhaki", "darkgreen")))   # 'vote' is a correlation matrix, not a data frame corrgram(vote, order=TRUE, upper.panel=panel.cor) #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter #> Warning: argument 1 does not name a graphical parameter    # outer labels, all options, larger margins, xlab, ylab labs=colnames(state.x77) corrgram(state.x77, oma=c(7, 7, 2, 2),          outer.labels=list(bottom=list(labels=labs,cex=1.5,srt=60),                            left=list(labels=labs,cex=1.5,srt=30,adj=c(1,0)))) mtext("Bottom", side=1, cex=2, line = -1.5, outer=TRUE, xpd=NA) mtext("Left", side=2, cex=2, line = -1.5, outer=TRUE, xpd=NA)

what: Draw a correlogram using `grid` graphics — corrgram2
title: Draw a correlogram using `grid` graphics — corrgram2
text: `corrgram2()` produces correlogram using grid graphics. -diagonal cells can shaded filled custom panel functions show correlation structure data matrix correlation matrix.

title: Draw a correlogram using `grid` graphics — corrgram2
code: corrgram2(   x,   type = NULL,   order = FALSE,   labels,   panel = grid_panel.shade,   ...,   lower.panel = panel,   upper.panel = panel,   diag.panel = NULL,   text.panel = grid_text.panel,   label.pos = c(0.5, 0.5),   label.srt = 0,   cex.labels = "fit",   dir = "left",   legend = FALSE,   col.regions = colorRampPalette(c("red", "salmon", "white", "royalblue", "navy")),   cor.method = "pearson",   title = NULL,   abs = FALSE )

title: Draw a correlogram using `grid` graphics — corrgram2
text: x data frame matrix one observation per row, correlation matrix. type Use 'data' 'cor'/'corr' explicitly specify whether `x` raw data correlation matrix. Usually inferred. order variables reordered? Use `FALSE` reordering, `TRUE` `"PC"` PCA-based angular ordering, `"seriation"` optimal seriation via `cba` package. labels Labels use diagonal instead column names. panel Default panel function used `lower.panel` `upper.panel`. ... Additional arguments passed panel functions. lower.panel, upper.panel Separate panel functions used diagonal. diag.panel Optional panel function used diagonal drawing diagonal labels. text.panel Included API compatibility [corrgram()]. Diagonal labels currently drawn [grid_text.panel()]. label.pos Horizontal vertical placement diagonal label. label.srt Rotation diagonal labels. cex.labels Label size diagonal labels. Use `"fit"` size labels panel width. dir Direction main diagonal. Use `"left"` `"\"` descending diagonal, `"right"` `"/"` ascending diagonal. legend `TRUE`, draw legend color scale. col.regions function returning vector colors. cor.method Correlation method passed panel functions. Default `"pearson"`. title Optional title drawn correlogram. abs Logical; `TRUE`, use absolute correlations variable reordering.

title: Draw a correlogram using `grid` graphics — corrgram2
text: Invisibly returns `NULL`.

title: Draw a correlogram using `grid` graphics — corrgram2
text: function grid-graphics variant [corrgram()]. accepts either data matrix/data frame one observation per row correlation matrix. raw data supplied, correlations computed `use = "pairwise.complete.obs"`. Variable reordering can used improve display placing related variables near . `order = TRUE` `order = "PC"` use PCA-based angular ordering described Friendly (2002). `order = "seriation"` uses `cba::seriation(..., method = "Optimal")` requires `cba` package.

title: Draw a correlogram using `grid` graphics — corrgram2
text: Friendly, Michael. 2002. Corrgrams: Exploratory Displays Correlation Matrices. *American Statistician*, 56, 316–324. http://datavis.ca/papers/corrgram.pdf D. J. Murdoch E. D. Chow. 1996. Graphical Display Large Correlation Matrices. American Statistician, 50, 178-180.

title: Draw a correlogram using `grid` graphics — corrgram2
code: # Draw a grid-based correlogram from data vars6 <- setdiff(colnames(auto), c("Model", "Origin")) corrgram2(auto[vars6], order = TRUE,     lower.panel = grid_panel.shade,     upper.panel = grid_panel.pie)   # 'vote' is a correlation matrix corrgram2(vote, order = TRUE, upper.panel = grid_panel.conf)

what: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via levelplot — levelplot_panel.ellipse
title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via levelplot — levelplot_panel.ellipse
text: functions provide custom panel methods lattice::levelplot().

title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via levelplot — levelplot_panel.ellipse
code: levelplot_panel.ellipse(   x,   y,   z,   subscripts,   at,   level = 0.9,   label = FALSE,   ... )  levelplot_panel.pie(x, y, z, subscripts, at = pretty(z), scale = 0.9, ...)

title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via levelplot — levelplot_panel.ellipse
text: x Numeric coordinates levelplot. y Numeric coordinates levelplot. z Correlation values levelplot. subscripts Subscripts lattice panel. (used) Breaks color levels. level Confidence level ellipse (default 0.9). label Logical; TRUE, show correlation values text. ... Additional arguments passed panel functions. scale Numeric; scaling factor pie size (default 0.8).

title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via levelplot — levelplot_panel.ellipse
text: levelplot_panel.ellipse Draws ellipses representing correlation coefficients upper triangle matrix. Optionally adds numeric labels lower triangle. levelplot_panel.pie Draws pie glyphs representing correlation coefficients, omitting diagonal.

title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via levelplot — levelplot_panel.ellipse
code: library(lattice) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘lattice’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:corrgram’: #>  #>     panel.fill levelplot(vote, at = do.breaks(c(-1.01, 1.01), 20),             xlab = NULL, ylab = NULL, colorkey = list(space = "top"),             scales = list(x = list(rot = 90)),              panel = levelplot_panel.ellipse, label = TRUE)  levelplot(vote, at = do.breaks(c(-1.01, 1.01), 20),             xlab = NULL, ylab = NULL, colorkey = list(space = "top"),             scales = list(x = list(rot = 90)),              panel = levelplot_panel.pie, label = TRUE)

what: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via splom — splom_panel.pie
title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via splom — splom_panel.pie
text: functions provide custom panel methods lattice::splom().

title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via splom — splom_panel.pie
code: splom_panel.pie(   x,   y,   z,   subscripts,   at = pretty(z),   cor.method = "pearson",   ... )  splom_panel.shade(   x,   y,   z,   subscripts,   at = pretty(z),   col.regions = NULL,   cor.method = "pearson",   ... )  splom_panel.ellipse(x, y, col.regions, cor.method = "pearson", ...)

title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via splom — splom_panel.pie
text: x Numeric coordinates levelplot. y Numeric coordinates levelplot. z Correlation values levelplot. subscripts Subscripts lattice panel. (used) Breaks color levels. cor.method Correlation method (default "pearson"). ... Additional arguments passed panel functions. col.regions Color palette shading (default NULL uses internal red white blue palette).

title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via splom — splom_panel.pie
text: splom_panel.pie Draws pie glyphs representing correlation coefficients, omitting diagonal. splom_panel.shade Draws shaded rectangles hash lines representing correlation coefficients. splom_panel.ellipse Draws ellipses representing correlation coefficients. position ellipse determined position data, shape ellipse determined correlation.

title: Panel functions for lattice corrgrams via splom — splom_panel.pie
code: library(lattice) pengvars <- c("bill_len", "bill_dep", "flipper_len", "body_mass") splom(~penguins[ , pengvars], upper.panel=splom_panel.pie, pscales=0)  splom(~penguins[ , pengvars]|penguins$species, upper.panel=splom_panel.pie, pscales=0)  splom(~penguins[ , pengvars], upper.panel=splom_panel.shade, pscales=0)  splom(~penguins[ , pengvars]|penguins$species, upper.panel=splom_panel.shade, pscales=0)  splom(~penguins[ , pengvars], upper.panel=splom_panel.ellipse, pscales=0)  splom(~penguins[ , pengvars]|penguins$species, upper.panel=splom_panel.ellipse, pscales=0)

title: corrgram 1.15 ()
text: Happy 20th anniversary package 2026. Add lattice panel functions make lattice corrgrams. Add corrgram2() make grid-based corrgrams. Documentation pages now created via Github Actions. Remove corrgram(..., font.labels) argument. Switch MIT license

title: corrgram 1.14 (2021-04-01)
text: CRAN release: 2021-04-29 seriation package moved Imports Suggests. makes corrgram lighter loading people. (Requested P.Kiener others) outer.labels argument improved default values.

title: corrgram 1.13 (2018-07-09)
text: CRAN release: 2018-07-09 Test coverage 95% using covr package. New panel.fill() function, omits diagonal lines. panel.conf panel.cor now auto-scale based absolute value correlations. panel.conf now allows cor.method="pearson" (default.) Fix issue #13.

title: corrgram 1.12 (2017-05-07)
text: CRAN release: 2017-05-07 New vignette invalid correlation matrix. corrgram() gives better warning symmetric matrix values outside [-1,1]. Added package logo github.

title: corrgram 1.11 (2017-04-03)
text: CRAN release: 2017-04-03 New argument outer.labels adding labels along outside edges corrgram. (Request Vanessa Bruat others.)

title: corrgram 1.10 (2016-11-09)
text: CRAN release: 2016-11-09 Function corrgram() now returns correlation matrix. Fixed custom label ordering order=TRUE M.Bruneaux. Began using testthat package.

title: corrgram 1.9 (2016-07-16)
text: CRAN release: 2016-07-16 New panel function panel.cor colored correlation values. Fixed minor bugs complete cases. Added cases tests directory.

title: corrgram 1.8 (2015-07-03)
text: CRAN release: 2015-07-03 Namespace changes due R devel changes.

title: corrgram 1.7 (2015-02-13)
text: CRAN release: 2015-02-13 Added seriate options.

title: corrgram 1.6 (2014-08-29)
text: CRAN release: 2014-08-29 Moved packages Depends Imports. Argument label.pos now defaults c(.5, .5) x,y positioning. (Request Evan Williams). New argument cor.method. (Request Evan Williams).

title: corrgram 1.5 (2013-08-29)
text: CRAN release: 2013-08-29 Updated references links. (Request Michael Friendly). Fixed small bug test correlation matrix. (Reported F. Rosa)

title: corrgram 1.4 (2012-11-07)
text: CRAN release: 2012-11-07 New argument col.regions specify panel colors. Re-worked examples section variety.

title: corrgram 1.3 (2012-08-15)
text: CRAN release: 2012-08-15 New panel function panel.bar. (Request dadrivr) Added example unclipped labels (test suite).

title: corrgram 1.2 (2012-03-28)
text: CRAN release: 2012-03-28 Small bug. Now accepts NA values correlation matrices, NAs caused missing combinations data cor(use=“pair”). Non-numeric columns data ignored. (Request JZ) New panel function panel.density. Test suite now includes corrgram inverse correlation, partial correlation matrices.

title: corrgram 1.1 (2011-10-20)
text: CRAN release: 2011-10-20 Added namespace.

title: corrgram 1.0 (2011-07-02)
text: CRAN release: 2011-07-02 New panel function panel.conf. New data set vote. New arguments dir, type, label.srt, title, abs. Now works either data.frame correlation matrix using type argument. New ordering method OLO using seriation package. Ordering can now done absolute value correlations.

title: corrgram 0.1 (2006-12-01)
text: CRAN release: 2006-11-30 First release CRAN.

title: corrgram 0.0 (2006-04-01)
text: Package development begins.
